reasons: Karrie pressures Grock's durability with Max HP true damage instead of relying only on raw physical damage.
She can punish Grock when he fails to instantly reach the backline.

proof.summary: Grock is durable and wants to absorb pressure, while Karrie can stack Lightwheel Mark and cut through frontline durability with Max HP true damage.
worksBestWhen: Karrie avoids being trapped by Grock's wall.
failureCases: Grock initiates with teammate follow-up before Karrie can stack damage.

reasons: Dyrroth can punish Grock in direct skirmishes by reducing Physical Defense and forcing Grock to respect melee burst.
This is most useful before Grock controls the terrain setup.

proof.summary: Grock is hard to burst through normal damage, while Dyrroth's defense-reduction pressure helps punish him in lane or small fights.
worksBestWhen: Dyrroth fights before Grock has a clean wall angle.
failureCases: Grock uses terrain and control-immunity timing to disengage.
Dyrroth fights inside Grock's team instead of isolating him.

reasons: Valir can push Grock back and slow his follow-up after Grock attempts to charge or zone.
Valir helps teammates reset when Grock tries to trap them with terrain.

proof.summary: Grock wants to force enemies into awkward paths, while Valir can use pushback and slows to break the engage timing.
worksBestWhen: Valir saves Searing Torrent for Grock's approach.
failureCases: Grock blocks Valir's retreat before Valir can cast.

reasons: Diggie can reduce Grock's engage value by giving nearby allies control immunity and shields during the commit window.
This is useful when Grock is paired with other crowd-control follow-up.

proof.summary: Grock wants his wall and charge to start a decisive fight, while Diggie can protect allies during the control window.
worksBestWhen: Diggie saves Time Journey for Grock's real engage.
Diggie stays near the teammates Grock wants to trap.
failureCases: Grock baits Time Journey with wall pressure.
Grock re-engages after Diggie's ultimate expires.

reasons: Lunox can threaten Grock with magic penetration and burst while having tools to avoid a bad engage.
She is more reliable when she does not have to walk into Grock's wall zone.

proof.summary: Grock's durability makes normal burst less reliable, while Lunox's penetration and defensive or blink options give her a better way to trade.
worksBestWhen: Lunox waits out Grock's wall placement.
failureCases: Grock traps Lunox before she enters the correct state.
Lunox is forced into Grock's teamfight zone.
